mnemonics: P vs NP: Possible vs Not Possible (to solve quickly)
NP-Complete: No Problem is Complete without verification
keyPoints: P problems are solvable in polynomial time, NP problems are verifiable in polynomial time
NP-Hard problems are at least as hard as the hardest NP problems
NP-Complete problems are both NP and NP-Hard, representing the most difficult problems in NP
commonMistakes: Assuming NP means 'not polynomial' (it means nondeterministic polynomial)
Thinking NP-Hard problems must be in NP (they don't have to be)
Believing all NP problems are equally difficult (they have different complexity levels within NP)
